architecture.summary: Llama SLayer implements a selective layer expansion and pruning strategy for post-pretraining knowledge injection in LLMs. The system evaluates layer importance via three metrics (angular distance, performance drop on layer removal, performance drop on layer insertion), then applies block expansion to shallow (first-half) layers while pruning deep layers. Expanded blocks are initialized via weight averaging of adjacent layers and fine-tuned during continued pretraining, while earlier blocks remain frozen. Information flows through the original transformer layers with strategically inserted expansion blocks in shallow regions only.
trained_components: expanded blocks in shallow layers (first half)
final layer (reinitialized from averaged discarded deep layers)

biblio.title: Llama SLayer 8B: Shallow Layers Hold the Key to Knowledge Injection

claims.statement: Shallow layers hold the key to knowledge injection; the degradation shrinks as the layer gets deeper after removal or expansion of shallow layers
Llama SLayer-8B achieves 19.03 on GSM8K, compared to Llama Pro baseline of 17.02
Llama SLayer-8B achieves 22.62 on HumanEval, compared to Llama Pro baseline of 20.95
Llama SLayer-8B-INSTRUCT achieves 56.25 on GSM8K compared to Llama Pro-Instruct baseline of 50.18
Llama SLayer-8B-INSTRUCT achieves 35.15 on HumanEval compared to Llama Pro-Instruct baseline of 32.92
Weight averaging initialization for expanded blocks produces lower performance drop (37.09 on TruthfulQA) compared to identity copy method (38.76) on MMLU and related benchmarks
Deleting up to 2 of the deepest layers is feasible without severely degrading model performance; deleting 4 layers degrades performance substantially
Llama SLayer-8B saves 5.5% training time cost compared to Llama Pro with slightly higher trainable parameters, on 30B token CPT dataset
